examples: Smoking is a causative factor in several major diseases.
Anisakis simplex larvae is the most important causative agent of anisakiasis.
The only well-established causative factor is cigarette smoking.
That required the arrival of Yersinia Pestis, the actual causative agent of the disease itself.
However, it is difficult to identify the causative agents in complex chronic diseases, which can take many years to develop.
